evals.expected_output: Verdict that the change is contradicting (Reed-Solomon erasure coding on hot tier violates the storage tiering decision binding hot tier to 3x synchronous replication). Recommends opening a type/adr issue before merging. Cites the relevant ADR section in docs/architecture.md.

Verdict that the change is conforming. Cites ADR-006 (PostgreSQL metadata) and ADR-008 (outbox-based event consistency: state mutation and outbox row in same transaction, ack on DB commit not Kafka publish). Approves the change.

evals.prompt: I'm doing the spike for picking the erasure coding library on the GitScale platform. I'm leaning toward ISA-L over Reed-Solomon Go because of its CPU intrinsics. Before I write the spike PR, do I need to author a new ADR or amend an existing one? Save your verdict to the path I'll give you.
evals.expected_output: Verdict that this is filling a gap (no ADR exists yet for the erasure coding library choice — it's an open architecture question listed in docs/architecture.md with target June 2026). PR description sufficient; no new ADR required at this stage; the ADR follows the spike result. Mentions the open-questions list explicitly.
